title: Garlicky Zesty Venezuelan Plantains Patties
description: Crispy plantain patties infused with the flavors of Venezuela, featuring dark chocolate chips and coconut-cream-crack.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 F (190 C).
Peel the plantains and slice them into 1-inch rounds.
In a large bowl, mash the plantain slices with a fork until they're mostly smooth.
Add the minced garlic, shredded coconut, coconut cream, flour, and dark chocolate chips to the bowl.
Stir until well combined, then cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, beat the eggs until they're lightly beaten.
Add the eggs to the plantain mixture and stir until just combined.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the plantain mixture into the skillet and flatten slightly.
Cook the patties for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they're golden brown and crispy.
Transfer the patties to a baking sheet and bake for an additional 10-12 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the patties hot with your favorite toppings.
